Name: v3: NakedClaude Demo
Description: Rulebook generated from Airtable base 'v3: NakedClaude Demo'.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable customer name.

schema.name: Notes
schema.Description: Free-form notes about the customer.

schema.name: IsStopped
schema.Description: True when the customer's current Status is a blocking status. Lookup of Statuses.IsBlocking.

schema.name: Status
schema.Description: Link to the customer's current Status in the Statuses table.

schema.name: StatusDisplayName
schema.Description: Human-readable display name of the customer's current Status. Lookup of Statuses.DisplayName.

schema.name: StatusDescription
schema.Description: Description of the customer's current Status. Lookup of Statuses.Description.

schema.name: Orders
schema.Description: Reverse link to all Orders placed by this customer.

schema.name: Email
schema.Description: Primary email address for the customer.

schema.name: Phone
schema.Description: Primary phone number for the customer.

schema.name: CompanyName
schema.Description: Company the customer represents, if applicable.

schema.name: BillingAddress
schema.Description: Default billing address for the customer.

schema.name: ShippingAddress
schema.Description: Default shipping address for the customer.

schema.name: CreatedAt
schema.Description: Timestamp when the customer record was created.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Lowercase, dash-separated compound key derived from DisplayName. Used as the natural key for the status.

schema.name: DisplayName
schema.Description: Human-readable display name of the status (e.g., "On-Hold", "In-Review").

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: Free-form description of what this status means and when it applies.

schema.name: IsBlocking
schema.Description: True when customers in this status should be considered stopped (blocked from progressing).

schema.name: Customers
schema.Description: Reverse link to all Customers currently in this status.

schema.name: SortOrder
schema.Description: Display order for this status in lists.

data.Name: delayed
data.Description: This status is used when the customer or request has been delayed and is not progressing as scheduled.

data.Name: cancelled
data.Description: This status means the customer or request has been cancelled and will not proceed further.

data.Name: processing
data.Description: This status means the customer or request is currently being processed.

data.Name: new
data.Description: This status indicates a newly created customer or request that has not yet been processed.

data.Name: in-review
data.Description: This status is for customers or requests that are currently under review.

data.Name: pending
data.Description: This status shows the customer or request is pending further action or review.

data.Name: on-hold
data.Description: This status indicates the customer or request is on hold and temporarily paused.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Lowercase, dash-separated compound key derived from SKU. Used as the natural key for the product.

schema.name: SKU
schema.Description: Stock-keeping unit identifier for the product. The natural compound key.

schema.name: OrderLineItems
schema.Description: Reverse link to all OrderLineItems that reference this product.

schema.name: DisplayName
schema.Description: Human-readable product name shown in catalogs and orders.

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: Long-form product description.

schema.name: UnitPrice
schema.Description: Default list price per unit.

schema.name: Cost
schema.Description: Internal cost per unit.

schema.name: IsActive
schema.Description: True when the product is currently sellable.

schema.name: StockQuantity
schema.Description: Number of units currently in stock.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Lowercase compound key combining the linked Customer and OrderNumber (e.g. 'bob-1001'). Used as the natural key for the order.

schema.name: OrderNumber
schema.Description: Sequential or human-assigned order identifier (e.g. '1001'). Combined with Customer to form the order's natural key.

schema.name: Customer
schema.Description: Link to the Customer who placed this order.

schema.name: OrderLineItems
schema.Description: Reverse link to all OrderLineItems on this order.

schema.name: Payments
schema.Description: Reverse link to all Payments applied to this order.

schema.name: OrderDate
schema.Description: When the order was placed.

schema.name: OrderStatus
schema.Description: Lifecycle state of the order.

schema.name: ShippingAddress
schema.Description: Where this specific order ships to.

schema.name: BillingAddress
schema.Description: Billing address used for this specific order.

schema.name: Notes
schema.Description: Free-form notes about this order.

schema.name: TaxRate
schema.Description: Sales tax rate applied to this order.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Lowercase compound key combining the linked Order and LineNumber (e.g. 'bob-1001-line-1'). Used as the natural key for the line item.

schema.name: LineNumber
schema.Description: Position of this line within its parent Order (e.g. '1', '2'). Combined with Order to form the line item's natural key.

schema.name: Order
schema.Description: Link to the parent Order this line item belongs to.

schema.name: Product
schema.Description: Link to the Product being ordered on this line.

schema.name: Quantity
schema.Description: Number of units ordered on this line.

schema.name: UnitPrice
schema.Description: Price per unit captured at the time of ordering (may differ from current Product.UnitPrice).

schema.name: DiscountPercent
schema.Description: Per-line discount applied to this item.

schema.name: Notes
schema.Description: Free-form notes about this line item.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Lowercase compound key combining the linked Order and PaymentNumber (e.g. 'bob-1001-pmt-1'). Used as the natural key for the payment.

schema.name: PaymentNumber
schema.Description: Sequential payment identifier within the parent Order (e.g. '1', '2'). Combined with Order to form the payment's natural key.

schema.name: Order
schema.Description: Link to the Order this payment is being applied to.

schema.name: PaymentDate
schema.Description: When the payment was received.

schema.name: PaymentMethod
schema.Description: How the payment was made.

schema.name: TransactionId
schema.Description: External processor transaction identifier.

schema.name: Notes
schema.Description: Free-form notes about this payment.

schema.name: Amount
schema.Description: Amount paid.

schema.name: PaymentStatus
schema.Description: Lifecycle state of the payment.

calc_orders_item_count
data.SQLCode:
-- FunctionOverride: calc_orders_item_count
-- Errors in this file are non-fatal (runs without ON_ERROR_STOP).

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.calc_orders_item_count(p_order_id text)
 RETURNS integer
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
BEGIN
  RETURN ((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM order_line_items WHERE "order" = p_order_id))::integer;
END;
$function$;

data.Name: calc_orders_last_payment_date
data.SQLCode:
-- FunctionOverride: calc_orders_last_payment_date
-- Errors in this file are non-fatal (runs without ON_ERROR_STOP).

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.calc_orders_last_payment_date(p_order_id text)
 RETURNS timestamp with time zone
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
BEGIN
  RETURN (SELECT MAX(payment_date) FROM payments WHERE "order" = p_order_id);
END;
$function$;

data.Name: calc_orders_payment_count
data.SQLCode:
-- FunctionOverride: calc_orders_payment_count
-- Errors in this file are non-fatal (runs without ON_ERROR_STOP).

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.calc_orders_payment_count(p_order_id text)
 RETURNS integer
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
BEGIN
  RETURN ((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM payments WHERE "order" = p_order_id))::integer;
END;
$function$;

_meta._CMCC_Summary: Airtable export with schema-first type mapping: Schemas, Data, Relationships (FK links), Lookups (INDEX/MATCH), Aggregations (SUMIFS/COUNTIFS/Rollups), and Calculated fields (formulas) in Excel dialect. Field types are determined from Airtable's schema metadata FIRST (no coercion), with intelligent fallback to formula/data analysis only when schema is unavailable.

_conversion_metadata.field_type_mapping: checkbox→boolean, number→number/integer, multipleRecordLinks→relationship, multipleLookupValues→lookup, rollup→aggregation, count→aggregation, formula→calculated

type_inference.priority: airtable_metadata (NO COERCION) → formula_analysis → reference_resolution → data_analysis (fallback only)
type_inference.airtable_type_mapping: checkbox→boolean, singleLineText→string, multilineText→string, number→number/integer, datetime→datetime, singleSelect→string, email→string, url→string, phoneNumber→string
type_inference.data_coercion_hierarchy: Only used as fallback when Airtable schema unavailable: datetime → number → integer → boolean → base64 → json → string
